title: First Non-Repeating Character in a Stream
description: You are given a stream of lowercase characters, arriving one by one.
After each new character arrives, you must report the first character in the stream that has appeared exactly once so far.
If no such character exists at that moment, report '#'.
Return the sequence of reports as a single string.
constraints: Stream contains only lowercase English letters

explanation: Step 2: 'a' arrives → now 'a' appears twice → no unique → output '#'
Step 4: 'c' arrives → 'b' is still the first unique → output 'b'

edge_cases: All characters repeat → output is all '#'.
Stream of length 1 → output is the same single character.
Long stream where many characters become invalidated late.
notes: Use a queue to track candidate unique characters in arrival order.
Use a frequency map to invalidate characters when they repeat.
Pop from the queue while the front is no longer unique.
